WORK INSTRUCTION – RECONFIG – DISCRETE PICKING

## **DISCRETE PICKING (JOBS)**

The Discrete Picking option is used to do the picks for an individual Job.

*Please note that you cannot mix items for different Jobs onto a single LPN (Xfer LPN)*

- **Step 1:** Using the Mobile Application, navigate to 'Picking'-> 'Manufacturing' -> 'Discrete Picking'
- **Step 2:** Enter the correct Organization and press 'Enter' on the keypad

**Step 3:** Equipment selection: Scan the Equipment barcode on your truck or scan the appropriate barcode displayed in the aisles.

Click on 'Done'

![](_page_0_Picture_10.jpeg)

- **Step 4:** Now you will see the first Pick Task on your scangun Scan the location barcode to confirm the subinventory/locator
- **Step 5:** In the 'LPN' field you only need to scan the LPN if it is available in the location Usually items that are not serialized do not have an LPN
- **Step 6:** In the 'Item' field scan enter the item number (make sure it is the correct item) Verify the UOM (do not change this)
- **Step 7:** In the 'Confirm' field confirm the pick quantity making sure you physically pick the correct quantity.

Note: If you pick an entire LPN the quantity is defaulted

- **Step 8:** The system will validate if the item is a serialized item and if the Serial Numbers need to be captured. If so, the system will display the 'SN' field enabling scanning of the Serial Numbers using the 2D or 1D barcodes (do not use the flashlight!)
  - If the Pick Task is for an entire LPN, there is no need to scan the Serial Numbers
  - When the Pick Quantity is less than the quantity on the LPN, or when the materials are unpacked then each Serial Number must be scanned. The scangun will count down for you.
- **Step 9:** In the 'Xfer LPN' field a new (pre-generated) LPN that you want pick into
  - If you are picking an entire LPN, the Xfer LPN will be defaulting that LPN
  - If this is not your first pick for the job then Oracle will default the suggested LPN All defaults can be changed.

Click on the 'Load' button to complete this Pick Task. This will automatically trigger a WIP Job Pick Label

The scangun will automatically display the next Pick Task for the job.

When no more tasks are shown and the scangun shows the Discrete Picking menu again with the message 'Group of picking tasks completed', then the picks for the job have been completed.

If your pallet is full, you can take your pallet with picked items to the Drop Zone.

## **CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION**

WORK INSTRUCTION – RECONFIG – DISCRETE PICKING

*Please note: In some cases the 'Exception Handling' process must be followed instead. For example when the location is empty, when the quantity in the location is not enough to satisfy the Pick Task, or when the location contains a different item.*
